# Creative Brief: doug-march.com
**Date:** April 27, 2026 | **Signal Influence:** 5/10 (Moderate)

---

## Mood

Monday morning in late April carries a quiet momentum—spring is now fact, not promise, and the daylight is genuinely generous (13.5 hours). The site should feel alert but not frantic, grounded in the actual season rather than chasing it. # Visual Specification — doug-march.com — April 27, 2026

**Archetype: The Stack**
**Chassis: playfair-outfit** (Playfair Display + Outfit)

---

## Why The Stack

The brief forbids Poster, Specimen, and Scroll — eliminating the single-column cinematic approach the PM described. The Stack is the closest structural analog: it gives sequential discovery through distinct band-beats, creates the "readable landmarks" the brief craves (Index undertones), and lets each of six signals live in its own en

output.feedback:
REVISE

- **Critical hue mismatch:** The spec claims H:115° but the provided hex values (`#F4F8EE`, `#E6EFD9`, `#587040`, etc.) calculate to H:80°–H:95°. This directly duplicates April-18's palette (H:82°, warm spring yellow-green Gallery Wall), violating the 20° separation standard without acknowledgment or justification. April-18 was Saturday-weighted (whitespace, design focus, waning moon gentleness); today is Monday-weighted (structured momentum, 13.5-hour daylight). These signals do not gen

output.rationale: This preset distills the visual specification's structural essence: a stack of five distinct bands, each with its own background and text tone, unfolding the portfolio as progressive revelation. The green-tinted neutral palette (H:120°, the mathematical center of the allowed 112°–128° band) creates "spring paper and forest shadow"—every neutral hex has R=B with G>R, mathematically guaranteeing the hue and preventing any warm contamination. A single saturated green accent (#22A022, S:64%, L:38%)
